mnemonics.title: SWITCH MEMORY AID
mnemonics.content: SWITCH: S-elect W-ith I-ntegral T-ype X-ecutable C-ases H-andled. REMEMBER: Switch only works with integers, so keep your CHARs and ENUMs in mind!

mnemonics.title: BREAK PREVENTION
mnemonics.content: To remember when fall-through is intentional: 'COMMA to the rescue - case 1,2,3: do same action'. Use comma to group cases without break.

mnemonics.title: DEFAULT POSITION
mnemonics.content: DEFAULT goes ANYWHERE but CONVENTION says END. Always include DEFAULT to catch the unexpected!

acronyms.expansion: Selection With Integral Type Cases, Handled
acronyms.usage: Use switch when you have multiple discrete values to compare against a single expression. Works best with int, char, enum types.
Each case label is a constant that the switch expression is compared against. Must be unique within the switch block.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a railway switch track with multiple branches. The expression value determines which track (case) the train takes. Without break, the train continues on all subsequent tracks!
Picture water flowing through pipes. Without break, water flows into all downstream pipes. With break, the water stops at that pipe and exits the system.
Think of a directory in an office building. Instead of checking each office sequentially, you go directly to the correct floor using the floor number - that's the jump table concept.

analogies.analogy: Like a menu in a restaurant. You tell the waiter (switch) your choice (expression), and they direct you to the specific dish (case). If nothing sounds good, you might get the chef's special (default).
Like exiting a电梯 (elevator) at your floor. Without break, you continue to every floor below. Break gets you out at the right stop!
Like an 'otherwise' clause in a conversation. If none of your specific responses apply, you have a fallback answer that handles any unexpected situation.
